message:
perf(storage): batch new-message inserts and cache daily_stats upsert keys

Two perf wins on the hot conversation-merge path, plus a benchmark that
exercises the case where a remote source is re-imported with new
messages appended to existing conversations.

Batched message inserts:

- Previously each new message went through franken_insert_message,
  which does a single-row INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(idx)... DO NOTHING and
  then queries last_insert_rowid. For 100-message conversations that
  meant 100 round trips through the sqlite VFS layer per merge.
- The merge paths already prove "this idx is new" before calling
  franken_insert_message (the dedup happens via pending_messages /
  pending_replay_fingerprints), so the ON CONFLICT clause was never
  load-bearing for those callers.
- Split franken_insert_message into a shared payload helper
  (franken_message_insert_payload) and a new franken_insert_new_message
  that does a plain INSERT (no ON CONFLICT). Add
  franken_batch_insert_new_messages which chunks at 100 rows (8 binds
  per row × 100 = 800, comfortably under SQLite's default
  SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER of 999), builds a multi-row VALUES clause,
  and reconstructs row IDs by subtracting from last_insert_rowid (only
  safe because the chunk is one autoincrement INSERT statement).
- Update the four merge sites (insert_messages_into_existing,
  upsert_conversation, upsert_conversation_profiled, and the import
  path's new-conversation branch) to collect the proven-new messages
  first, then run a single batch insert and zip the returned IDs back
  with the original messages for snippet/FTS/analytics follow-up.
- Reorder the pending_messages / pending_replay_fingerprints inserts
  so they happen before the batch call, preserving the previous dedup
  semantics across messages within a single conv.

daily_stats per-(day,agent,source) upsert cache:

- franken_update_daily_stats_in_tx fires four ON CONFLICT upserts per
  call (agent×source, all×source, agent×all, all×all). For sources that
  drop hundreds of conversations in one merge, the same four (day_id,
  agent_slug, source_id) keys get re-upserted continuously even though
  the row already exists after the first call.
- Add EnsuredDailyStatsKey + an Arc<Mutex<HashSet<_>>> on FrankenStorage
  that is shared across cloned writers (the existing
  ensured_conversation_sources cache uses the same pattern). Rename the
  open_writer_with_shared_sources / new_with_shared_sources constructors
  to *_with_shared_caches and thread the new cache through.
- Introduce franken_apply_daily_stats_delta_in_tx: when the cache
  reports the row already exists, run a plain UPDATE (no ON CONFLICT
  parsing, no INSERT plan); otherwise fall back to the upsert and
  record the key on success.
- Group the per-call session/message/chars deltas into a StatsDelta
  struct so the four target rows share one Copy value instead of
  passing the same triple of i64s six places.

Benchmark coverage:

- Add bench_append_remote_source_merge to integration_regression with
  600-conversation warmup, then iterates re-merging conversations whose
  message count doubles. Runs both the persist_conversation path and a
  direct insert_conversation_tree variant for each of {5, 20, 50}
  message counts so future regressions in either the batch insert or
  the daily-stats cache surface immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
